description: This 3D color-coded surface plot presents the variation of the stress softening coefficient $k_s$ (color scale range: 0.2 to 1, higher value indicates weaker softening) for low-alloy steel under multi-stage hot deformation conditions, associated with dynamic recrystallization (DRX) behavior. The two horizontal axes denote the natural logarithm of the Zener-Hollomon parameter of the first deformation stage ($\ln Z_p^{(I)}$) and second deformation stage ($\ln Z_p^{(II)}$), both ranging from 22 to 36, while the vertical axis represents the first-stage DRX fraction $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ ranging from 0 to 1, with four sliced surfaces at different $\varphi_d^{(I)}$ values for visualization. Visual observation confirms that $k_s$ decreases (softening effect enhances) with increasing difference between $\ln Z_p^{(I)}$ and $\ln Z_p^{(II)}$, and with rising $\varphi_d^{(I)}$, consistent with reported results. This figure quantifies the dependence of stress softening behavior on two-stage deformation parameters and intermediate DRX fraction, supporting the development of high-accuracy constitutive models for flow stress prediction under non-constant hot deformation conditions.
